chunk_summary: This subtopic outlines mandatory supplementation guidelines for vegan children to prevent nutrient deficiencies, focusing on vitamin B12, protein, minerals, vitamin D, calcium, iodine, and omega-3 fatty acids, with specific dosages and dietary strategies tailored to age and risk factors.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of vitamin B12 supplementation in vegan children and breastfeeding mothers.
Learn strategies to ensure adequate intake of protein, iron, zinc, calcium, and vitamin D in vegan diets.
Recognize the role of iodine and omega-3 fatty acids in vegan children’s nutrition and how to address deficiencies.
Apply national prophylaxis schedules and pediatric guidelines for managing deficiencies in vegan children.
key_facts: Vitamin B12 supplementation is mandatory: daily supplement or fortified foods delivering age-appropriate doses.
Vitamin D: 400 IU/day in infancy; risk-based supplementation in older children.
Calcium: Use fortified plant products and food sources like ragi and sesame; supplement if intake is inadequate or rickets suspected.
Iron and zinc: Follow national IFA prophylaxis and treat confirmed IDA promptly; vegan diets do not prevent anemia.
Omega-3: Emphasize daily ALA sources (flax, chia, walnuts) and consider algae-derived DHA in restrictive diets.
Iodine: Confirm iodized salt use; avoid exclusive use of non-iodized salts.
reference_quote: Vitamin B12 is non-negotiable. Require either (1) a daily B12 supplement, or (2) verified intake of fortified foods that deliver an age-appropriate dose every day.
document_key_topics: Vitamin B12 supplementation for vegan children
Bone health: Vitamin D and calcium strategies
Iron, zinc, and anemia prevention in vegan diets
Iodine and omega-3 planning for vegan children
